summary: Data shared by Ookla shows AT&T's switch from Nokia to a 'single vendor' deal with Ericsson has delivered network improvements.

title: Nokia rolls out agentic AI for home and broadband networks
summary: Nokia has introduced new agentic AI capabilities for its fixed network product lines that aim to boost operational efficiencies and accelerate fiber rollouts. #pressrelease

title: FCC OKs EchoStar spectrum sales with $2.4B escrow that EchoStar didn't want
summary: The FCC's approval of EchoStar's spectrum sales to AT&T and SpaceX requires that EchoStar set up a $2.4 billion escrow account for claims from infrastructure companies that believe they are owed money from Dish Wireless.

title: Harmonic still reliant on Comcast and Charter, but customer base is diversifying
summary: Comcast and Charter represented more than half of Harmonic's broadband revenues in Q1 2026, but the vendor is seeing some growth in its 'rest of market' segment.

title: Lumen expands its US network with NorthLine, new northern fiber route
summary: Seattle to Minneapolis route will deliver 100G and 400G wavelength services to support large-scale traffic flows across a growing US corridor. #pressrelease

title: GTT EnvisionDX accelerates quoting for secure networking with real-time pricing available for 11M global locations
summary: Enterprises can now get global pricing in minutes as GTT continues to simplify and automate complex network and security services. #pressrelease

title: NTT updates medium-term plan with AI, data center focus
summary: NTT seeks to achieve a consolidated EBITA of $25.38 billion by 2030 with a revised medium-term plan focusing on AI and data centers.

title: Gaiia raises $40M
summary: Gaiia has raised $40 million in a Series B round led by JMI Equity, with participation from Inovia Capital, to further modernize its AI-native operating system for communications service providers. #pressrelease

title: Teleste and Vecima tout 'open' DAA platform for European ops
summary: Vecima and Teleste have introduced an open and interoperable distributed access architecture for European cable operators that matches Vecima's virtual CMTS with Teleste's remote PHY node portfolio, plus local sales and support. #pressrelease

title: Scandal-hit Asian telcos seek to regain trust
summary: KT's earnings dive as it admits it needs to win back customer confidence, while other Asian telcos face similar challenges.

title: AOI nets role in Mediacom's DOCSIS 4.0 upgrade
summary: AOI said Mediacom is tapping into the vendor's 1.8GHz amps, line extenders and monitoring software to support the operator's DOCSIS 4.0 network upgrade. #pressrelease

title: Vodafone hails new chapter, but Germany remains a work in progress
summary: Vodafone CEO Margherita Della Valle says the group is now in good shape and plans to focus on organic growth in future, while it continues to face challenges in Germany.

title: Comcast claims utility company is violating FCC pole replacement order
summary: Comcast is urging the FCC to step in amid claims that Appalachian Power Company is violating a first-of-its-kind order related to how much cost the operator must bear for certain pole replacements.

title: Broadband advocates pick apart FCC's proposed Lifeline reforms
summary: In comments filed to the FCC, groups including Public Knowledge and the National Digital Inclusion Alliance (NDIA) said the Commission's proposed Lifeline reforms 'risk further lowering the program's participation levels.'

summary: Fueled by rising demand for 1.8GHz amps, AOI expects cable-related revenues to exceed its original projections for 2026.

summary: EchoStar lost another batch of pay-TV subs amid much slower Boost subscriber gains in Q1. The company also reiterated that its financial future hinges on multi-billion-dollar spectrum sales to AT&T and SpaceX.
